level.description: Best-fit career level inferred from the resume.

yearsExperience.description: Approximate total years of professional experience.

primaryDomains.description: Domains where the candidate is strongest, e.g. 'semiconductor test', 'BI/data analytics'.

coreSkills.description: Concrete tools/technologies/methodologies the candidate clearly owns.

preferences.description: Types of roles the candidate is well-suited for, in plain English.

antiPreferences.description: Types of roles that would be a poor fit (e.g. RTL/circuit design, intern).

education.description: Each formal degree as its own string, most recent first: degree level, field/major, institution, and years — e.g. 'M.S. Materials Science, Harbin Institute of Technology (2014-2016)'. Many postings gate on a degree (e.g. 'MS in CS/EE or related field'), so capture level and field accurately. Empty array only if the resume genuinely lists no education.

headline.description: One-sentence elevator pitch for the candidate.
